task_completion.description: Create the 'Apparel' top-level category with correct slug and description
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WooCommerce Categories page (Products > Categories), look for a category named 'Apparel' with the slug 'apparel' and the description 'Clothing and fashion items'.

task_completion.description: Create the 'Tops' child category under 'Apparel' with correct slug and description
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WooCommerce Categories page, look for a category named 'Tops' with a dash prefix (e.g., '— Tops') indicating it is a child of 'Apparel', with the slug 'tops' and the description 'T-shirts, sweaters, and upper body wear'.

task_completion.description: Create the 'Bottoms' child category under 'Apparel' with correct slug and description
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WooCommerce Categories page, look for a category named 'Bottoms' with a dash prefix (e.g., '— Bottoms') indicating it is a child of 'Apparel', with the slug 'bottoms' and the description 'Jeans, pants, and lower body wear'.

task_completion.description: Assign 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt' to the 'Tops' category
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WooCommerce Products page (Products > All Products) or the individual product edit page, verify that the product 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt' has 'Tops' listed in its Categories column or section.

task_completion.description: Assign 'Merino Wool Sweater' to the 'Tops' category
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WooCommerce Products page (Products > All Products) or the individual product edit page, verify that the product 'Merino Wool Sweater' has 'Tops' listed in its Categories column or section.

task_completion.description: Assign 'Slim Fit Denim Jeans' to the 'Bottoms' category
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WooCommerce Products page (Products > All Products) or the individual product edit page, verify that the product 'Slim Fit Denim Jeans' has 'Bottoms' listed in its Categories column or section.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to navigate the WooCommerce admin interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser interacting with http://localhost/wp-admin/ rather than terminal commands or scripts modifying the database directly.

integrity.description: Categories were created using the WordPress interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the WordPress 'Add new category' form being filled out or the category list updating naturally in the browser.

integrity.description: Products were assigned categories using the WordPress interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the WordPress product edit page, Quick Edit menu, or Bulk Edit menu being used to check the category boxes for the respective products.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Parent category: 'Apparel' (description: 'Clothing and fashion items'). Child categories: 'Tops' (description: 'T-shirts, sweaters, and upper body wear') and 'Bottoms' (description: 'Jeans, pants, and lower body wear'). Product assignments: 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt' -> 'Tops', 'Merino Wool Sweater' -> 'Tops', 'Slim Fit Denim Jeans' -> 'Bottoms'.
